items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Abby!

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Ugh... I-I can't take it!

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Go chase your fate... Augusta...

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: Brazen faithless one!

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: Receive the judgment of the heavens

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: The sacred crucible shall reshape your soul

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Fool. I already said Ragunna's "Divinity" didn't get a seat on the ship

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: A hard-won battle... wasn't it?

options.text: And yet, victory still chose our side.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Yes...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: It's all thanks to you, {PlayerName}—Septimont's Hero of Heroes.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: In that case… I could say the same to you.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: As the prophecy goes, "The Hero of Heroes wielding extraordinary power, rising from the dark tides." As I see it, you, Augusta, fit that image well.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Extraordinary power? That's a bit much. I know my limits.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But… I do think I've earned that title, and it's one we could share together.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Besides, the Temple never said there could only be one Hero of Heroes.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What really happened to you in the Dark Tide? I thought you might've...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Hehe, it wasn't a pleasant experience. But in a strange way… it was refreshing.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: If you want to hear the full story, I'll tell you another time.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But, back inside the Dark Tide, I think the lost frequencies were always guiding me, shaping who I was becoming.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Even trapped in that abyss of despair, those fallen heroes never gave up. They lit a faint, warm light in the dark for me.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: And you, along with everyone who stood by me… without you, we couldn't have won this battle.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Perhaps the title of "ruler" is too narrow and too hollow to define the true spirit of humankind.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: A civilization's glory is forged by generations of brave souls. It was never meant to be one person's medal of honor.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I want to ask you something.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Why did you ask me to join the hunt?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Because my warriors and I wanted to fight beside someone like you. That hasn't changed.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But… I will be honest. Part of it was because of your "unwritten fate."

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I'd been preparing for this hunt for a long time. At first, all we had were a few vague glimpses from the Priestesses and a handful of prophetic murals. No one knew when or how the disaster would strike.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: And then you showed up. You've surely seen the unfinished mural in the Temple. The blank wall and the unwritten fate...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: So I took a gamble, believing you were the key to this hunt.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I couldn't explain it back then, not when I wasn't certain myself.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But in the end, we realized what that "blank" represents. And my gamble paid off.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The one who deserves to be a Hero of Heroes, with power over the Earthrend Wedge...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Thank you for your trust, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Now, it's time for me to honor my promise.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: This is the end of our journey... the Earthrend Wedge.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: It hasn't been quite what I expected, but I'm very glad we made it this far together.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Imperator...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Go. I'll wait for you here.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Did you find what you seek?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: A discoid? Looks like... a compass?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The structure's odd... There might be a hidden compartment inside. Can you try opening it, {PlayerName}?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: If this relic was left by the Sentinel, brute force won't get us anywhere.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: We'll need a different approach. Maybe a special key, a mechanism, or a password...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Any ideas?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The Black Shores' records on the Sentinel may give us more clues.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: More importantly, I need their data on the Dark Tide.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: The Earthrender's holding back the Dark Tide for now, but we're not in the clear yet. Those sudden outbreaks and the way it's fixated on you… it's not just a coincidence.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: You're right. With the Black Shores' tech, we might be able to outwit our unseen enemy.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'll contact them as soon as we're back.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: There's a friend of mine who… well, she knows a lot about Imperator. She might be able to help solve this mystery.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: More importantly... her powers are unique. The Earthrender's holding back the Dark Tide for now, but those sudden outbreaks and the way it's fixated on you… it's not just a coincidence.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: If anyone can put an end to the Dark Tide's threat in Septimont, it's her.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Sounds like someone worth meeting.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Do introduce me later. I'm curious what kind of person she is.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But one thing I'm sure of, she must be a formidable opponent and a figure to be respected, like you.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You two share a lot in common, actually. I think you'd get along.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'll reach out to her once we're back. She wasn't able to travel before due to some unavoidable reasons, but now, I think the timing's right.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Alright. Let's set all this aside and enjoy the victory we've earned.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: There's probably a bonfire roaring at the encampment by now… {PlayerName}, it's time to return to those waiting for us.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: We won this battle, but the war is far from over. The road ahead's still steeped in blood and thorns. But with you, and everyone who stood beside us, the banner of Septimont will never fall.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: With the eternal sun as my witness, the name of hero belongs to you all.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: The Wastelands...

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: I never imagined that after four years, I would return to this place under such circumstances...

items.speaker: Cowardly Gladiator
items.text: How did this place get like this…?

items.speaker: Veteran Gladiator
items.text: This is where the Dark Tide started sixteen years ago. The fact that this empty shell still stands is a miracle in itself.

items.speaker: Cowardly Gladiator
items.text: Does this mean... if we fail to stop the Dark Tide, Septimont too will...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Warriors, do not let fear stand in our way. The untarnished glory of Septimont stands behind us.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The devastation that befell Murmurstown sixteen years ago will never be repeated.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Because now, we are here.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The Sovereign won't make things easy for us. Any rash action before we can confirm its location and status would be unwise.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Your command is our duty, Your Excellency. We will deploy Anchor Stones around Murmurstown, and I'll personally see that the perimeter stays undisturbed.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Yes. This will trap Sovereign inside the town.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Good. Proceed as planned, Avidius.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Now, then. It falls to us to track down the Sovereign's whereabouts, {PlayerName}. Time is short, so we'll need to split up.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Keep your guard up. Those <te href=750019>Hollow Figures</te>, do not be fooled by their stillness. Those are just the shell. And watch for their songs of corruption.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: They were once our own—brave Gladiators, loyal citizens of Septimont.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The Dark Tide not only twisted their minds, it also raised their corpses and turned them into mindless puppets. Not even death could set them free.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Mark my words, it will pay for this malevolence.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Augusta...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: That's me.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Heh, guess there's no denying it now. I thought I might keep my foolishness as a child from you.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: So Murmurstown... I mean, Fabianum, is your hometown?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Yes. It's been a long time. I never imagined I'd see my past like this.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Hmm... I'm curious...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: About Manes Deep and the Oak Hollow?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Yeah!

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: ...The name Fabianum once stood for glory.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: As Septimont's frontline against the Dark Tide, it held the honor of receiving legends like Fabius, Atilius, and Valeria.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Perhaps that's why Hero of Heroes tales always lingered here. The most popular one was the tale of a great journey to Manes Deep, the ruins of an ancient civilization.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: It was said that if you brought "two lives"—yourself and your sword—you'd be granted entry. And within, you'd hear the unceasing whispers of the legendary heroes.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Whispers... they say, capable of awakening a Resonance ability as mighty as the heroes' own.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: As for the Oak Hollow said to lead to Manes Deep... just children's tales. No one ever knew if it was real.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But Fabianum never found its Hero of Heroes... Ever since then, the name was lost, replaced by Murmurstown, a scar that Septimont refuses to face...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The whole town held out until the very end, yet no aid ever came from the City of Septimont.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Later, I heard from the senior Gladiators that on the very morning Fabianum fell, most of the Senate lords were still sound asleep in their warm beds...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Heh... it wasn't the Dark Tide that doomed my hometown, but those in power who were blinded by greed and failed to act.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Forgive me. I let my emotions get the better of me.

options.text: It's okay. I feel like I've discovered another side of you.
It's okay. I can tell you've always been very sincere.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I see. Then perhaps I should spend more time with you. Like Iuno once did.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Anyway...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Why did the Sonoro reveal all this to us?

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Maybe it's helping us find that... um, pure Dark Tide frequency? The scent does feel clearer now, less muddled.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Or maybe... it meant to show us something more.

options.text: (Tell Augusta about the Unyielding Gladiator's Spirit.)

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: A soul who endured the Dark Tide's corruption... and still fights to speak the truth?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Hmm... his spirit deserves to be remembered. I will go to witness that truth with my own eyes.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The Dark Tide paid me no mind. Its target was always Angel.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: And the whispers… they were real, but not as the legends promised. What I heard were words of cruelty.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: None of it matters now. Those things belong to the past...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I was a reckless child, a hatchling flailing with a sword I could barely hold.

options.text: But you've grown stronger ever since.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: True... though, back then, I didn't really have a choice.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: This is Septimont, a place where ideals bow to strength.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: As a child, I had no desire for power. But in the City of Septimont, if I wished to rise beyond my origin and silence arrogance dressed in fame and fortune, I had no other path.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Now, my strength speaks in my stead, and my blade cuts down every slander.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: All but prejudice.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I know well what those parasites in the Senate are plotting. Draped in the Temple's name, they manipulate public opinion to secure themselves a place on Capitoline Hill.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But their absurd schemes betray only fear—

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Too afraid to face me, they skulk in the shadows, hoping empty prophecies might bring me to my knees.

options.text: So... you don't believe in the prophecies?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: It's not about belief. It's about not fearing them.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The Dark Tide still haunts Septimont. People live in terror of losing their homeland again, and so they cling to the hope of a Hero of Heroes.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: As Septimont's Ephor, I must stay strong and answer their call.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: A ruler has no right to falter.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: If my Resonance ability raises doubts about my right to rule, then let the true Hero of Heroes step forth and challenge me.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: And if none appear... I will decide whether this title is worthy of the weight of a ruler's glory.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: One of the prophecies speaks of a Hero of Heroes wielding extraordinary power, rising from the dark tides. As I see it, you, {PlayerName}, fit that image well.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Should it be you... I would gladly accept your challenge.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: A person of immense strength is either a nemesis or a kindred spirit, both of which are intriguing to me and my blade.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Me? Honestly, the thought never crossed my mind.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: From what I've seen, being a true ruler isn't about having immense strength, whether that's a powerful Resonance ability or sheer might.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I've met people from Huanglong to Rinascita who live with real integrity. Their skills and backgrounds were all different, and... none of them were perfect.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: But what defines them isn't strength or anything that can be measured. It's the spirit they were born with. Resilient. Unyielding. That can't be stolen.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Believe me, Augusta, I see that same spirit in you.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I see...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Iuno once said something similar to me.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: ...What happened within the Oak Hollow remains a mystery. All I know is, when I found her, her Resonance ability had grown immensely, so powerful that even the Dark Tide could not withstand it.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But she failed to control it. I could do nothing but watch as she transformed into... something no longer human. After that, I never saw her again.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: A Dark Tide occurrence sixteen years ago... Its frequency must have been more stable than it is now.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Maybe this is the truth the Gladiator's spirit is trying to share...

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Please hurry, everyone. The Anchor Stone must be in place before the Sovereign's hunt begins.

items.speaker: Shrewd Senator
items.text: Such devotion. Forgive me, but you seem to forget your place, Avidius.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Is that so? Perhaps you'd like to remind me, my lord.

items.speaker: Shrewd Senator
items.text: Hmph. You must be aware of your rather underwhelming performance during the Tyrannos hunt.

items.speaker: Shrewd Senator
items.text: The Senate's patience is not boundless. It's reserved for those who deserve it. Unless you can present a triumph to match your exploits in Manes Deep, we'll have no choice but to... reconsider your candidacy as Septimont's next ruler.

items.speaker: Shrewd Senator
items.text: Wake up, Avidius. Isn't Murmurstown the very place where your glory began? That, Avidius, is your true arena.

items.speaker: Shrewd Senator
items.text: Yet here you are, entangled in Augusta's petty concerns when you could be seizing something far greater.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: How very amusing, Lord Falco.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: You seek a ruler strong enough to lead, yet pliable enough to be led. You'd have better luck finding a needle in the sea.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Were I in your position, I'd keep my mouth shut. If Septimont falls, so will your ambitions.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: And allow me to remind you: I entered Manes Deep because I had no other choice, and my duty as a Gladiator demanded it.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: The blood I shed, the blade I wielded—these were proof of loyalty, not entertainment for the lords. If that's how you see it... then perhaps it is I who should reconsider this alliance.

items.speaker: Shrewd Senator
items.text: How dare you—

items.speaker: Veteran Gladiator
items.text: Heh, got some grit, eh? Haven't let ambition cloud your mind. I like that.

items.speaker: Veteran Gladiator
items.text: Falco? What are you—

items.speaker: Shrewd Senator
items.text: *Groans in pain*

items.speaker: Veteran Gladiator
items.text: Watch out!

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: ...He may be under the Hollow Figures' influence. Judging by his behavior, the Dark Tide has already corrupted his mind.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: The Anchor Stone is nearly in place. Sir Hercle, would you please take Lord Falco to rest?

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: This isn't far from the entrance to Manes Deep. I remember finding Mesona nearby. It's an herb that helped my companions and me resist the Dark Tide's corruption.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: ...Lord Falco has paid for his pride. But as a former hero, he deserves a death free from the Dark Tide's torment.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: I will do what I can to help...

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: Sir Hercle, please look after him. I'll bring the herb back.

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: I promise it won't take long. Don't worry.

items.speaker: Veteran Gladiator
items.text: Alright. You go do what you believe is right.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: This is the place! The smell's getting stronger!

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: No traces of the Sovereign... It must be hiding again.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: We must force it to show itself.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Perhaps this could work.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: ...Over there.

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: Y-yeah...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: But my belly... Ugh... it's full...

items.speaker: Abby
items.text: I'm... so tired...

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: Faithless one... The strength you refused shall now become the blade that strikes you down...

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: To spurn a divine gift is to invite consequence, and Divinity offers mercy but once...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Augusta...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Augusta.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Iuno, I...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: No, it can't end like this...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: All able units! Split into two teams! One will escort the wounded back to base, while the other prepares supplies and maintains position!

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Nothing… Just something on my mind.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Do you still remember what the whispers told me sixteen years ago?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: "The Dark Tide answers only to strength. Those without it are doomed to be forsaken..."

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Now I think of it, it is rather interesting...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I've never told anyone this before.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But ever since the Dark Tide decided to "forsake" me, those broken whispers have never left.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: They echoed in every battle, telling me when to dodge, when to strike. But with each opponent that fell, the whispers began to fade.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Until I became the Ephor...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: ...That's when they returned. Louder, never giving me a single moment of peace.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I tried to shut them out, but every time I strayed from their guidance, fate would strike me down in the cruelest of ways.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I thought I was the master of my fate, but the truth is... they always knew the path better than I ever could.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: ...The old prophecy about Ragunna that they once whispered to me has come true.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Now, it is time for me to put their power to the test.
It's been a while, Rinascita.
What prey do we hunt this time?
...The Threnodian.

items.speaker: Young Augusta
items.text: H-Hey, you! Evil monster!

items.speaker: Young Augusta
items.text: ...Why don't you fight me!

items.speaker: Young Augusta
items.text: I... I'm not scared! Come on, I'm wide open.

items.speaker: Young Augusta
items.text: Don't look away! I'm not someone you can ignore!

items.speaker: Young Augusta
items.text: No—

items.speaker: Young Augusta
items.text: Not there! Look at me! I said LOOK AT ME!

items.speaker: Young Augusta
items.text: Angel... Angel!

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: The Dark Tide answers only to strength. Those without it are doomed to be forsaken...

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: Augusta, my brave yet fragile child… Your strength is far too feeble, unfit to sate the hunger of the Dark Tide.

items.speaker: "Whispers"
items.text: You long to become a hero, to reign by might… But tell me... can the throne of glory be claimed by one so weak?

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Boss, what's going on?! I got a bad feeling just now, and Tethys won't stop pumping out alerts.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Aha… figures. That area you're in has buried many warriors. For the Sovereign, it's like having a free buffet.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: And sure enough, you blink and it's already swallowed up all the surrounding frequencies. Now it's a real nightmare.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: That said…

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: The situation's bad, no doubt. But we're not out of moves yet... The Dark Tide isn't spreading at full speed. If we act fast and find the Sovereign's weak spot, we might just pull a miracle for Rinascita.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Let's see… Fire under earth, light be blocked... That's it. Tethys is showing messy internal frequencies and unstable structures. Looks like after stuffing itself with all those frequencies, the Sovereign's completely scrambled inside.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: If we can stir up the mess in its gut, even with something small, we might just throw him off balance. And then, maybe we can wrest the Earthrend Wedge back and readjust it to—

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Terrible timing... Is there any other way to reach her?

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: It's the Dark Tide...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The entire frequency range is under its control. In other words, we're completely cut off here.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Tch, how vexing.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: The Dark Tide's inside...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: If we feed that monster something else…

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Right, the Anchor Stone!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You're suggesting... we let the Sovereign absorb the Anchor Stone? If it works, it could suppress it from the inside.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Very sharp. But before we act, there are two things we need to prepare.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: First, we'll need to get the Sovereign to open up and swallow the Anchor Stone like a good boy. That will be on you and Augusta, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Second, the Dark Tide itself must be stabilized to keep the Sovereign nice and solid. My job, naturally. No one else is fit for the task.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: No!

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The Sovereign is far stronger than before, and you've only just recovered. There's no way you can take on that much Resonance energy at once.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I'm not made of glass, Augusta.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I am Iuno, the most gifted Priestess Septimont has seen in generations. If anyone can hold back the Dark Tide, it's me. And I'm not going to fall on you that easily.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Besides, the Sacred Flames already showed me my end. In the warmth of spring, surrounded by white flowers, with gentle eyes watching over me. But that day is still far from today.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: At worst, I'll just need more time to recover. Everything's going to be alright. I promise.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Hmm...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Then it's settled. I'll let you prepare. Just give me a signal when you're near the Earthrender.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I won't be able to hold the Sovereign's form for long, so make the most of that window.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Did you forget anything, {PlayerName}?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Useful, isn't it? Keep it close. No matter where you go, a part of me goes with you, got it?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Since I've given you a tool so practical, I'll ask something in return. Just one thing.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Promise me you'll stay by Augusta's side, and face whatever destiny sets upon you. Together. This isn't a command or a suggestion. It's... a personal request.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Only you can do this.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Iuno...

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I think I understand what the "unknowable void" in your fate really means.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: It's not nothingness, {PlayerName}. All along, it's represented a future that can't be defined by anything but your own will.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: As long as that void exists, you control your story. Your existence is the meaning itself.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: So please, support Augusta with everything you have. I've seen how much she trusts you.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: So trust her in return. No matter what happens, you are not to doubt her in the slightest.

options.text: Augusta and I already agreed to walk this path till the very end.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: That's the spirit.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Alright, you two. Stop standing around and get moving.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: The sooner you settle this, the sooner I get to leave this boring place.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Iuno, one last question.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: For someone without an unwritten fate... is it true they can never break free, no matter how hard they struggle?

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I... don't know.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: That's alright.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Let's go, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Wait, Augusta!

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: I've been thinking... our final fate might already be written, but how we walk that path is up to us. We, as humans, are meant to carve our own way forward!

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: If we can't rewrite fate, then we chase it! Outpace it! Then, when we catch up, we'll smack it right in the face!

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Don't forget what you once said: "A ruler assumes their place by accomplishing the impossible for their subjects."

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: ...How could it be...

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: No... It must be the Dark Tide's illusion...

items.speaker: Avidius
items.text: This wall... No, it can't be...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: My fellow Gladiators! Our prey has finally revealed itself. Move to the marked coordinates. It's time to hunt.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: What a beautiful ending, my near-perfect protagonist. I must confess... even I didn't see this coming.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Should this be your final act? No. No, allow me this small indulgence, as your creator.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: The quill of fate may be sharp and cold, but still, I want to see how far the soul I created can go.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: After all, it's the stories that sink deepest into the mire that stir the heart most, don't you think?
